title: Hadith 1: Intentions
arabic: إِنَّمَا الأَعْمَالُ بِالنِّيَّاتِ، وَإِنَّمَا لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مَا نَوَى، فَمَنْ كَانَتْ هِجْرَتُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ فَهِجْرَتُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ، وَمَنْ كَانَتْ هِجْرَتُهُ لِدُنْيَا يُصِيبُهَا أَوِ امْرَأَةٍ يَنْكِحُهَا فَهِجْرَتُهُ إِلَى مَا هَاجَرَ إِلَيْهِ
english: Actions are judged by intentions, and every person receives according to what they intended.
questions: What is the main lesson about intention?
Who narrates from whom, if available?

questions.prompt: Which word completes this phrase: إِنَّمَا الأَعْمَالُ بِـ ____؟
questions.explanation: The phrase means actions are judged by intentions: بِالنِّيَّات.

title: Hadith 2: Islam, Iman, Ihsan
arabic: أَنْ تَشْهَدَ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، وَتُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةَ، وَتُؤْتِيَ الزَّكَاةَ، وَتَصُومَ رَمَضَانَ، وَتَحُجَّ الْبَيْتَ إِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلًا... أَنْ تَعْبُدَ اللَّهَ كَأَنَّكَ تَرَاهُ، فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُنْ تَرَاهُ فَإِنَّهُ يَرَاكَ
english: The hadith teaches Islam, Iman, Ihsan, and that Allah sees His servants.
questions: What are Islam, Iman, and Ihsan?
Who narrates from whom, if available?

questions.prompt: Which word completes this phrase: أَنْ تَعْبُدَ اللَّهَ كَأَنَّكَ ____؟
questions.explanation: Ihsan is to worship Allah as though you see Him: كَأَنَّكَ تَرَاهُ.

title: Hadith 3: The Pillars of Islam
arabic: بُنِيَ الإِسْلَامُ عَلَى خَمْسٍ: شَهَادَةِ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، وَإِقَامِ الصَّلَاةِ، وَإِيتَاءِ الزَّكَاةِ، وَحَجِّ الْبَيْتِ، وَصَوْمِ رَمَضَانَ
english: Islam is built upon five foundations: testimony, prayer, zakat, Hajj, and fasting Ramadan.
questions: What are the five foundations mentioned?
Who narrates from whom, if available?

questions.prompt: Which word completes this phrase: بُنِيَ الإِسْلَامُ عَلَى ____؟
questions.explanation: The hadith says Islam is built upon five foundations: عَلَى خَمْسٍ.

title: Hadith 4: Human Creation
arabic: إِنَّ أَحَدَكُمْ يُجْمَعُ خَلْقُهُ فِي بَطْنِ أُمِّهِ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمًا نُطْفَةً، ثُمَّ يَكُونُ عَلَقَةً مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ، ثُمَّ يَكُونُ مُضْغَةً مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ، ثُمَّ يُرْسَلُ إِلَيْهِ الْمَلَكُ فَيَنْفُخُ فِيهِ الرُّوحَ
english: A person's creation is formed by Allah in stages, and Allah knows each person's decree.
questions: What does this hadith teach about Allah's knowledge and decree?
Who narrates from whom, if available?

questions.prompt: Which phrase completes this phrase: فِي بَطْنِ ____؟
questions.explanation: The hadith describes creation in the womb of one's mother: فِي بَطْنِ أُمِّهِ.

title: Hadith 5: Rejected Innovations
arabic: مَنْ أَحْدَثَ فِي أَمْرِنَا هَذَا مَا لَيْسَ مِنْهُ فَهُوَ رَدٌّ
english: A religious matter introduced without basis in the religion is rejected.
questions: What is the main lesson about following the Sunnah?
Who narrates from whom, if available?

questions.prompt: Which word completes this phrase: فَهُوَ ____؟
questions.explanation: The hadith says an unsupported religious innovation is rejected: فَهُوَ رَدٌّ.
